title: Rich Fluffy Ethiopian Teff Bao
description: A sugar‑free, vegetarian Ethiopian-style fluffy bao made with teff flour, filled with a savory lentil‑spinach mix, perfect for a solo dinner.

ingredients.name: spice blend
ingredients.notes: turmeric 1 tsp, berbere 1 tsp, nutritional yeast 1 tbsp, black pepper 1/4 tsp

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together teff flour, all‑purpose flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add warm water, vegetable oil, and egg to the dry mixture; stir until a soft dough forms.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions, shape each into a small disk, and place on a parchment‑lined baking sheet.
Bake the buns for 20–22 minutes, or until lightly golden and puffed.
While the buns bake, sauté carrots and spinach in a bit of oil until softened.
Add cooked lentils, spice blend, and a splash of water to the vegetable mixture; simmer until the filling is heated through and slightly thickened.
Remove buns from oven and let cool for 5 minutes; then cut a small slit on the side of each bun to create a pocket.
Fill each bun with the lentil‑spinach mixture, serve warm, and enjoy your fluffy, nutritious dinner.
